what_it_does: Implements a stateless SubprocessAdapter that resolves an executable from a data-driven locator (env override, PATH via shutil.which, then known_paths) and runs a capture-style subprocess command for discover-time use.
observations: find_executable resolves in order: env_override, PATH which fallback, known_paths
find_executable raises RuntimeError with a detailed message if resolution fails
run_capture calls subprocess.run with stdout=PIPE, optional stderr merge, optional stdin string, and a default 15-second timeout
Returns a subprocess.CompletedProcess; fail-loud semantics are left to callers
Docstring explicitly scopes this to DISCOVER-time invocation, not held-open sessions
Uses from __future__ annotations and a string forward reference for subprocess.CompletedProcess
No platform-name literals appear in the implementation
standouts: Fail-loud executable resolution with explicit precedence chain
Clear separation between discover-time capture and live session supervision
Zero platform-name literals policy stated in the module docstring
conventions: Data-driven by an external locator object rather than hard-coded platform logic
concerns: find_executable repeats getattr calls and could produce a confusing error message if locator.name is missing
run_capture has no encoding argument and relies on text=True default locale
No validation that cmd is non-empty before subprocess.run
Timeout is hardcoded to 15 seconds with no caller override beyond the parameter default
No logging or structured diagnostics for invocation attempts
notes: A small, single-responsibility adapter that locates and runs subprocess commands for introspection discovery, deliberately staying out of live session management.
questions: What is the exact shape of the locator object passed to find_executable?
Which discoverers call run_capture and how do they handle non-zero return codes?
Are known_paths expected to be absolute, and do they need executable-permission checks?
Is the 15-second timeout sufficient for slow-starting platforms?
How should callers handle locale/encoding issues with text=True?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches implementation: it is a discover-time subprocess primitive with executable resolution and generic capture, not a session supervisor.
apparent_themes: Separation of discover-time and live-session concerns
intention_signals: Provide one reusable, deterministic subprocess invocation primitive so discoverers and version probes do not each re-implement executable lookup or capture logic.
novelty: The explicit 'zero platform-name literals' policy and the deliberate boundary between this discover adapter and a separate session supervisor.
contribution: A reusable, stateless subprocess invocation primitive with deterministic executable resolution and generic capture semantics for discover-time tooling.
summary_for_embedding: SubprocessAdapter is a discover-time subprocess invocation primitive that resolves an executable from a data-driven locator and runs capture-style commands with configurable stderr merging, stdin, and timeout.
intention_for_embedding: Intended to centralize executable lookup and run-to-completion capture for introspection discoverers while leaving held-open session handling to a supervisor.

symbols.does: Defines a stateless subprocess invocation adapter with a kind tag and two operations: executable resolution and capture invocation.
symbols.description: SubprocessAdapter is the discover-side subprocess primitive. It carries invocation_kind='subprocess' and exposes find_executable and run_capture, keeping platform-name literals out of the implementation.

symbols.does: Resolves an executable path/name from a locator using env override, PATH lookup, and known paths, raising RuntimeError if none is found.
symbols.description: find_executable implements a deterministic precedence chain: first an environment-variable override, then shutil.which on the locator name if fallback is enabled, then each known path expanded via os.path.expanduser. It fails loudly with a descriptive message rather than returning None.

symbols.does: Runs a command to completion, capturing stdout and optionally merging stderr, with optional stdin input and a timeout.
symbols.description: run_capture is a thin, generic wrapper around subprocess.run that returns a CompletedProcess. It leaves error and empty-output interpretation to the caller, making it suitable for discoverers and version probes that define their own parse rules.
